Company News

BeiLuo Launches Dedicated FAE Support Program for Infineon Customers

2024-08-10 By BeiLuo Editorial Team

Technical support has long been a differentiating factor in the industrial semiconductor distribution market. While many distributors can ship components quickly, fewer can offer the engineering expertise that helps customers design those components into working systems. As an authorized Infineon distributor serving customers across China and the Asia-Pacific region, we have formalized our technical capability with the launch of a dedicated Field Application Engineer (FAE) Support Program.

This program represents a significant investment in our customer support infrastructure. It establishes clear service levels, expands the scope of technical assistance we make available to customers, and provides a structured pathway for engineers to receive the expert guidance they need throughout the product development lifecycle.

What Is a Field Application Engineer

A Field Application Engineer (FAE) is a technical specialist who bridges the gap between semiconductor product capabilities and real-world application requirements. Unlike a standard sales representative, an FAE possesses deep knowledge of the product families they support -- including device architecture, application circuits, software drivers, and evaluation tools. We deploy our FAEs to help customers succeed in their designs, not just to process orders.

In the context of Infineon products, an FAE must understand a broad portfolio that spans power MOSFETs, IGBTs, gate driver ICs, automotive-grade microcontrollers from the AURIX family, power management ICs, and wide bandgap semiconductors such as silicon carbide (SiC). Each product family requires specialized knowledge of circuit design practices, thermal management considerations, and regulatory requirements.

We have built our FAE team over several years through targeted recruitment of engineers with backgrounds in power electronics, embedded systems, and automotive design. Our new FAE Support Program formalizes this team's engagement with customers, ensuring that every customer has access to qualified technical assistance regardless of order size.

The BeiLuo FAE Support Program Key Features

We have structured our FAE Support Program around three tiers of engagement, designed to match the level of support to the customer's specific needs.

Our Standard tier provides access to our online technical resource library, including Infineon application notes, reference schematics, simulation models, and evaluation board guides. Customers at this tier can also submit technical questions via our support portal, and we provide responses within two business days.

Our Professional tier adds direct access to a named FAE who is familiar with the customer's product line and design environment. Professional-tier customers receive priority response times, can schedule one-on-one video consultations for design reviews, and are eligible for on-site visits at our expense for complex design challenges.

Our Enterprise tier is designed for high-volume customers and strategic partners. Enterprise customers receive dedicated FAE coverage with proactive engagement, including quarterly design audits, early access to new Infineon product qualification data, and participation in our customer advisory board, which provides input on our inventory planning and service roadmap.

Technical Services Offered by BeiLuo FAEs

Our FAEs provide a range of technical services across the product development lifecycle. At the earliest stage, we assist customers with component selection, helping engineers identify the right Infineon product for their application based on electrical requirements, environmental constraints, package compatibility, and long-term supply availability.

During schematic review and PCB layout, our FAEs check for common design pitfalls such as gate drive undersizing, bootstrap circuit errors, thermal resistance calculation mistakes, and layout issues that could cause electromagnetic interference (EMI) or instability at high switching frequencies. Catching these issues early, before prototype fabrication, can save our customers weeks of debugging time and significant prototype costs.

At the prototype stage, we assist with evaluation board setup, oscilloscope measurement interpretation, and efficiency optimization. For customers using Infineon's iMOTION motor control ICs or AURIX microcontrollers, our FAEs can guide the configuration of Infineon's software tools, including the iMOTION Design Studio and AURIX Development Studio. This hands-on software support reduces the learning curve for engineers who are new to these platforms.

For customers preparing for production, our FAEs review design-for-manufacturing (DFM) considerations and check that the selected Infineon components are positioned correctly in Infineon's product lifecycle, helping customers avoid designs built around products that may be approaching end-of-life within the next two to three years.

Industries and Applications Supported

Our FAE Support Program covers the full range of applications served by Infineon's product portfolio. In industrial automation, our FAEs support customers designing servo drives, variable-frequency drives, programmable logic controllers (PLCs), and robotics controllers. For renewable energy customers, we assist with solar inverter designs, wind power converters, and energy storage system power electronics.

In the automotive and EV sector, our FAEs support customers working on onboard chargers (OBCs), DC-DC converters, battery management systems (BMS), electric power steering (EPS), and domain controller platforms based on AURIX MCUs. Our automotive FAE team understands the additional requirements imposed by IATF 16949 quality management and ISO 26262 functional safety standards.

For IoT and consumer applications, our FAEs help customers integrate Infineon XENSIV sensors, PSoC 6 microcontrollers, and OPTIGA security solutions into connected devices and smart-home products. We believe the breadth of Infineon's portfolio, combined with our technical depth, means that customers can rely on a single distributor for components and support across multiple product categories.

How to Engage BeiLuo FAE Support

Customers wishing to engage our FAE Support Program can begin by visiting our support page. New customers can register for Standard-tier access immediately. Customers with production volume requirements or complex technical challenges are encouraged to contact our customer success team to discuss Professional or Enterprise tier options.

We believe that strong technical support is not a luxury but a prerequisite for successful customer outcomes. By formalizing our FAE Support Program, we reaffirm our commitment to being more than an Infineon distributor. Our goal is to be a true engineering partner, helping customers bring better products to market faster and with greater confidence in the reliability of their Infineon-based designs.

Engineers who have already worked with our FAE team report that early engagement during the design phase consistently reduces prototype revision cycles and accelerates the path to volume production. For Infineon customers seeking that kind of partnership, our new FAE Support Program provides a clear and accessible framework for accessing it.
